place: Buena borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: The borough’s name, inspired by the 1847 Battle of Buena Vista during the Mexican‑American War, reflects its connection to American history.
Founded in 1948 and originally part of Buena Vista Township, Buena has deep historical roots, influenced by early land developer Charles K.
In 1999, Buena celebrated its 50th anniversary with the inaugural "Buena Day" at Bruno Melini Park, an event that continues to honor the borough’s growth and unity.
Padre Pio Shrine, established in 2002 in the Landisville section, attracts visitors seeking spiritual reflection.
It is also included in the greater Philadelphia‑Reading‑Camden metropolitan area, making it a welcoming and accessible place to live and visit.
Over the years, Buena has blossomed into a community known for its strong local ties and cultural events.
